{{Quote|Oh, no. A vorshak. Those pesky bark-eaters don't live around here.|Weechee Warrick, upon seeing a vorshak in Bright Tree Village|<ref name="Blue Harvest" />}}
'''Vorshaks''', informally nicknamed "'''bark-eaters'''," were a type of [[Hair/Legends|furry]], [[Limb/Legends|quadrupedal]] [[Species/Legends|creature]] that lived on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. They had powerful front [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] that allowed them to consume the [[Tree/Legends|trees]] of the [[Forest/Legends|forest]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], one vorshak was captured by a [[Gorneesh's tribe|tribe]] of [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]] and set loose in the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks']] [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], which it began to devastate. The Ewok [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] managed to tame the vorshak with a [[love potion]] and save the village.

==Biology and appearance==
Vorshaks were [[Limb/Legends|quadrupedal]] [[Species/Legends|creatures]] that inhabited the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]],<ref name="Blue Harvest" /> a world in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Outer Rim Worlds">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> They had thick coats of [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]], with dark patches surrounding a pair of dark [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Vorshaks were distinguished by their bushy tails and large, powerful front [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]].<ref name="Blue Harvest" />

==Behavior==
{{Dialogue|Wicket Wystri Warrick|The vorshak will destroy the support trees.|Chirpa|We better tell the warriors to ready their weapons.|Logray|Wait. There is another way.|attr=Wicket Wystri Warrick, Chirpa, and Logray, shortly before taming a vorshak with a love potion|src=<ref name="Blue Harvest" />}}
Vorshaks were [[Diet/Legends|herbivorous]], consuming [[Tree/Legends|trees]] of the [[Forest/Legends|forest]]. The Endor-[[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] considered this behavior to be "pesky," and they referred to vorshaks as "bark-eaters." Vorshaks demonstrated no signs of [[Sentience/Legends|sentience]], and they could be tamed with [[love potion]]s.<ref name="Blue Harvest" />

==History==
{{Quote|Come on. While the Ewoks play with the vorshak, we'll relieve them of their harvest.|Gorneesh, to his tribe|<ref name="Blue Harvest" />}}
[[File:Vorshak-BlueHarvest.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A vorshak was set loose in Bright Tree Village.]]
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date note">{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated series, which include the appearance of a vorshak in "[[Blue Harvest]]," to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the Endor-residing [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]] led by [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]] planned to [[Thief/Legends|steal]] the seasonal harvest from their cousins, the Ewoks of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. Following a scheme devised by the Dulok [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Umwak]], the Dulok [[Gorneesh's tribe|tribe]] captured a vorshak and brought it to Bright Tree Village. The vorshak began to eat the trees of the village, distracting the [[Bright Tree Village tribe|Ewok tribe]] and giving the Duloks an opportunity to steal their harvest.<ref name="Blue Harvest" />

When the Ewoks [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] and [[Weechee Warrick]] heard the vorshak in the village, they gathered with other village members to watch the creature. When Wicket noted that the vorshak was eating the village's support trees, the tribe's [[Chief/Legends|chief]], [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], suggested readying [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] to confront the creature. However, the Ewok Shaman [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] suggested another solution and had [[Gender/Legends|his]] apprentice, [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], fetch a love potion. Logray doused a net with the potion, and instructed Wicket and Teebo to throw the net over the vorshak.<ref name="Blue Harvest" />

The vorshak was caught in the net and resisted, before becoming susceptible to the potion. Once the potion took effect, the vorshak was tamed and started to roll and prance through the forest. When the Duloks noticed what had happened and realized the Ewoks would be returning, they retreated.<ref name="Blue Harvest" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vorshaks first appeared in "[[Blue Harvest]],"<ref name="Blue Harvest" /> the twelfth episode of the animated television series [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']]. The episode was written by [[Paul Dini]] and [[Sam Wilson]], and it premiered on [[November 23]], [[1985]].<ref name="Insider 27">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref>

==Appearances==
*{{Ewoks|Blue Harvest}} {{1st}}
